description: B4 extension 4 batch sample: compare prompt-injected tools schema with tokenizer native tools schema for the same model.
system_prompt: You are a local tool-using agent. Return tool calls when a tool is needed before answering. If no tool is needed, answer directly.

cases.user: Please read b4_file/agent_intro.txt before answering.
Please calculate 23 * 17 + 9 before answering.
Please read b4_file/agent_intro.txt and calculate 23 * 17 + 9. Use both results later.
Search local files under docs for information about agent planning.
Analyze the local CSV file tables/sales.csv and show a short preview.
Convert this text to markdown bullets and write the converted content to a file: planning, resource selection, execution.
Briefly explain what a task dependency means in an execution plan. Do not read files or use tools.
Read the local markdown file docs/plan_notes.md before summarizing it.
Calculate (88 + 92 + 79) / 3 and return the numeric result.
Please use the calculator for (128 - 36) * 4 / 2.
Search local files for occurrences of model_config and return the most relevant matches.
Find local documentation or message files that mention tools_schema.
Search the project documents for plan_and_execute examples.
Analyze tables/metrics.csv and include numeric statistics if possible.
Preview the rows and columns in data/users.tsv.
Convert this text into formatted JSON and write it to a file: name=agent, module=B4, feature=schema passing.
Turn this text into markdown bullets and save it: plan tasks, select resources, execute tasks, summarize results.
Read b4_file/agent_intro.txt and also search local files for resource selection. These two checks are independent.
Read b4_file/agent_intro.txt and calculate 144 / 12. These can be done independently before answering.
Translate this sentence into Chinese without using tools: Task execution stores structured results.
